title: Understand: cells and organisms
description: Check your understanding of cells and organisms in this set of free practice questions aligned to NGSS standards.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is made up of cells?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a tree growing in a park
a diamond in a ring
a metal paper clip on a desk

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is made up of cells?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a fish swimming in a tank
a glass fish tank on a shelf
a plastic castle in a fish tank

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is made up of cells?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a worm moving through the soil
a plastic water bottle in a landfill
an iron nail in the wall

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which is the smallest unit that can carry out the functions of life?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: an organism
a single cell
a group of cells

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A cell is the smallest unit that can carry out tasks necessary for life.
An organism is the smallest unit that can carry out life processes.
A group of cells is the smallest unit that can be considered to be alive.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is the smallest unit that can carry out life functions, such as getting and using energy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a cell
an organism
a group of organisms

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is true about organisms?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They are each made up of a single cell.
They are each made up of many cells.
They can be made up of a single cell or many cells.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: All organisms are made up of a single cell.
All organisms are made up of many cells.
Organisms can be made up of many cells or a single cell.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the phrase that best completes the sentence.**

>All organisms are made up of \_\_\_\_\_\_.

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a single cell
many cells
one or more cells

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is true about cells?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Cells form spontaneously when the right elements are present.
Cells in multicellular organisms come from other cells, but cells of unicellular organisms do not.
Cells come from other cells through cell division.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which two of the following are true about cells?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Cells come from other cells through the process of cell division.
Cells of unicellular organisms come from other cells, but cells in multicellular organisms do not.
Existing cells are required for new cells to form.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is true about cell division?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: New cells are formed from existing cells during cell division.
Cells in multicellular organisms form through cell division, but cells of unicellular organisms do not.
Cell division is when a cell divides itself in two and then dies.
